title: ferray-stats: sort/argsort axis defaults to None (flatten) — NumPy defaults to -1 (last axis)
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

NumPy's np.sort(arr) sorts along the last axis by default (axis=-1). ferray-stats sort() takes axis: Option<usize> where None means 'flatten and sort'. This is a semantic difference: NumPy users expect np.sort(matrix) to sort each row independently, but ferray users get a flattened 1D result. The default should be Some(ndim-1) for the last axis, with explicit None to flatten. Also, no negative axis support (#434 pattern).
